title: The creator economy is dying thanks to AI
author: Dan Koe

sections.title: What Is The Dead Internet Theory?
sections.content: According to Wikipedia,the dead internet theory is a conspiracy theory suggesting that the internet died, sometime around 2016 to 2017,and is now largely dominated by bots and AI generated content rather than people.But there's something even more surprising about this theory that nobody's talking about.Now, I'm not sure why we still label this as a conspiracy theory,because most of the internet is made up of bots and AI content, and it will only get worse.But honestly, I don't really see this as a problem.In fact, it could present one of the greatest opportunities for creatives, and entrepreneurs and founders and everyone else to stand out.On the other hand,there's an endless supply of hyper addictive braindead, content that people can scroll through at any given time.It's a little insane to me, but the average screen time for the average American is a little over seven hours.And sure, some of that may be work related, but we all know that the majority of it, even if you're at work, is spent on social media.Reports of depression, uncertainty and stress have gone through the roof,especially with the threat of AI replacing human, jobs, leaving us grasping at our last sources of purpose.Even when most people say they hate their jobs.The thing is, people don't want to change.People become so accustomed to pain that it feels like home and leaving.It seems like the real suffering.And with AI breaking record high benchmarks every other week, individuals, and companies can churn out hundreds to thousands of pieces of content every day.Now, since this content is so addicting, the stress and depression and uncertainty, and anxiety will only continue to get worse unless we learn to control ourselves.The question is, is this actually a problem because we've all thought about it?Is society really going to end up like that scene from Wall-E where everyone is obese,floating around in a chair, being drip fed sugary beverages and entertainment all day long? I'm not so sure processed food is bad.

sections.title: Processed Vs. Organic Content
sections.content: That's obvious.We all know it.But that doesn't mean we can't have a cookie or two.Or maybe an entire butter cake to yourself at 1 a.m.watching old episodes of The Office after a few birthday espresso martinis, to balance out the overall optimization of our lives.The problems start to spring up when we can't control ourselves for an extended period of time, and that's very easy to do.Fast food chain streaming companies, gaming companies and social media companies, pour hundreds of billions of dollars into optimizing dopamine, driven feedback loops designed to keep consumers addicted.Facebook alone spends over $20 billion annually on research and development.Now, what makes fast food particularly addictive, is this brutal combination of fat, sugar and salt.These were scarce resources that humans aren't adapted to have, in abundance in today's world were adapted for survival.Scarce ingredients, when found, spurred dopamine into our brain to signal that we found something to aid in our survival. And once companies discovered this mechanism, they could now test and create combinations, of these ingredients that kept consumers addicted and coming back for more.The internet companies did the same thing.They adopted an advertising model for monetization, and soon found that, polarizing and inflammatory headlines led to capturing the most attention.Now, all they had to do was serve more of what users liked to an instantly accessible screen.And that was the birth of the For You page.Now there's processed food, and there's also processed, content, digital fat, sugar and salt, cheap dopamine,instant gratification, entropy, quick and shallow content is just that in tropic.The more you consume, the more chaotic your mind becomes.There is no long term sense making, just a bunch of useless ideas, filling your brain without any goal or vision to apply them to.The digital food doesn't energize you or build muscle.It makes you feel so sluggish.You just want to lay down all day and gain fat. Being a Wall-E citizen, drooling over a curated algorithm that feeds you short, term, gratifying content keeps you in a state of stress and survival.The combination of fear and desire pull you away from the present moment.In psychic entropy or disorder in the mind increases what you thought to be one harmless, post was actually a combination of 50 posts you read in unison that silently induces chaos.You regress.You stay the same.You lock yourself into a paradigm of political ideologies and static opinions, that aren't conducive to achieving anything worthwhile in your life.The sad thing is, you don't care, and you probably didn't even realize you got yourself into this situation.Or you did, and you forgot about it shortly after and then just got back into the situation.I'm the same. It happens to all of us.That's why I'm creating this video.But there is a bright side to this.You already know that you can't trust social media companies to change their incentives, but you can and must take matters into your own hands.

sections.title: Shop At Digital Farmer's Markets
sections.content: Now, this is a tweet the other day that I saw from Kevin on Twitter,and it was called thoughts on Building a Personal Brand.And based on my other videos, like The Death of the personal brand and a lot of the one person, business videos that I've made.I agree with a lot of his points over optimize profiles equals, Tryhard equals low status chasing engagement trades off authenticity.Writing about others is lame and has no moat.I see this all the time.People just write about other people, rather than build authority and talk about ideas that they like or share their interests or skills.Being an expert is overrated. Just be yourself in public.Being a hypocrite for your team equals you're a loser.In other words, if someone that you like says something, that you don't agree with, but you agree with them, you're a loser.Reputation be to reach every time and posting, should be downstream of doing and the most important one.If you even try to build a personal brand, you are by definition. less authentic than someone who doesn't try to build one yet has it.We're going to go over some examples of these soon, but it's just a great way to frame, what we're going to talk about in terms of the difference between creators in AI generated content.And the point with me saying that is that those kinds of personal brands, the people that shape, what's posted on the internet because the AI content is following the trends, that the personal brands and the influencers create, but they can't replicate that beautiful authenticity.So shop at digital farmers markets, because on the flip side of processed food, and content, there's organic food and content, and organic content is central.Unlike entropy, that leads to disorder and chaos, entropy leads to order and clarity.But you can't expect the for you page to feed you this content.You must actively search, curate and nurture your digital feed, especially as I content's starts to ramp up.Now, how do you spot organic content?First, it is useful. It aids in the achievement of your goals.The caveat here is that you actually have to have self-generated goals.If you don't have a vision.Most content aside from that which helps generate vision is useless.Go read a philosophy book.The second thing is that it is often long form, books, articles, newsletters, podcasts and YouTube videos.It has enough room to deliver something of sense and value.However, the thing here is that many short form creators can post, insights and education that act as a small puzzle piece for your mind.So there is short form centric content, but if it doesn't act as a puzzle piece, if it's just an idea floating off, and it doesn't connect to anything you want to do in your life, then it's not doing you any good.The third thing it is authentic.It is not created because it has to be.It is created because the creator deems it valuable enough to share with an audience that may benefit from it.One last thing here is that organic or delayed gratification content. can still use attention and marketing tactics to hook your attention in.That's the game you have to play.Technically, any book that hooks your attention is using these tactics, whether they're trying to or not.That's how you create an interesting story is by doing those things.So the attention grabbing headlines and stuff isn't the problem.It's the content within it.It's whether or not that content is actually suited for you and what you want to do in your life.So you aren't just being manhandled around and being programed, by your digital feed to want things that you don't actually want.And that's what makes you feel lost.And I have to say it here, but if you want to learn how to write these, articles, newsletters, posts that are central, that actually help people,check out the writer's Bootcamp, which is a cohort, that we run along with our writing software cortex.And by popular request, the next one starts on March 4th.So enroll before then because it will close on that date. The point with all of this is that social media is not bad by any means.It's actually an incredible way to tap into an infinite source of knowledge because where else can you find, information from people who have done what you want to do in your life?Most successful people are self-educated, and most self-education today is done through a screen.Royalty and Roman civilization like Marcus Aurelius, had access to the best teachers to prepare them for the throne.And now that you have access to the best teachers online, you decide to scroll videos of hot and dumb people dancing.Make it make sense.

sections.title: Why AI Content May Be A Good Thing
sections.content: Nobody's talking about the fact that AI generated content could actually be a good thing.Because here's the thing it doesn't matter if the internet is flooded, with a ton of garbage content, it already has been for years now.Even further, if this actually posed a problem, to the social media companies, as in people didn't like it,so the social media company started to lose revenue, they would be able to solve it.They could use cryptography or biometric passes to prove that you're a human when online,but it clearly hasn't gotten to be enough of a problem for them to do that yet, because it's not a problem.It's fun to whine and complain about this dead internet, reality to distract you from what you can do about it.You're worried that these bots and AI generated content, have removed your ability to find the information you want to find on the internet,when that's not the case at all, that couldn't be any further from the truth. Most people just want most things fed to them, and the social media companies are giving them just that.As I mentioned in the beginning, we become so accustomed to pain that it starts, to feel like home, and leaving that pain feels like the real suffering.On top of that, think about how people actually view content.There's a ton of content. Nobody's arguing about that.But you can only view so much at any given point in time, and you can only follow so many people.The reality here is that AI does not generate good content on its own.I've tried to get it to replicate my writing.I've engineered simple prompts and prompts the size of a mini course on social media,and out of 100 posts that the AI spits out to me, maybe one can be posted.The rest are useful ideas that I completely rewrite.Some could use minor edits, but there's a crucial point here AI content isn't good unless you are good at content.Most of it goes straight to the bottom of the barrel and isn't seen by human eyes. The dead internet is like an iceberg, and the majority of the dead content is so deep that nobody will ever see it.And if the AI content was actually good, would it matter if I were to create this exact video, word for word, scene by scene with AI?Would it matter if it gave you the value that you were looking for?I would guess that it wouldn't.You wouldn't care.But to that you may say, oh well, if I knew it was AI, then something would feel off.And that worry stems from a fundamental misunderstanding of what AI is.Because hype, the hype around AI, all these new models are hitting new benchmarks,so all the jobs are going to be replaced.It's very good at clouding perception, because intelligence, is only one resource, and it's proving to be a pretty poor one.In isolation, it falls flat if there is no vision, taste, or agency.If you aren't already good at the thing, I won't be good at the thing.If I created this video with AI versus someone else, the I can't just do that on its own. I would have to orchestrate everything from the images, the scenes, everything else.It's still technically me creating the video.I'm just not in front of a camera anymore, and I can create them, faster and distribute the knowledge that I have faster.And that's just the thing.I hasn't replaced content.It's just made bad content easier to create.And people don't care about that.The fact remains that if you are inexperienced and unskilled and you're trying to use, AI to get a quick result like you have been with the get Rich quick courses and everything else you can get your hands on, to not commit to something long term centric that brings meaning and purpose to your life, you're going to fail.The principles haven't and won't change, ever.But Dan, the first jobs that AI is going to replace are social media managers, copywriters, and content marketers.Correct. They're going to replace the formulaic one.You know, the social media managers who post a fancy image meeting Canva. that gets absolutely zero results for the client, or the content marketer who puts a picture of a pretty girl on a screen, with a Gen Z caption that people are already tired of, and this can already be done with something like Real Farm,or the copywriter who posted blog posts for that, one company and got fired because I could do it faster.If you're writing simple blog posts, to just allow the company to have an online presence than you, now you're pretty replaceable.Think of AI as a ghost writer, or a ghost, designer, or a ghost coder, or a ghost filmmaker.A ghost writer doesn't create the entire story, they just write it.James Patterson, and this is a shock to most people, uses a ghostwriter for his novel.The thing is, James still orchestrates, the plot, the characters, the lore, the marketing, the sales, the business, and the rest.The ghostwriter simply strings this together in writing.And that's exactly what I will do, not just for writing, but for all of the above. Allowing Patterson to do this all by himself if he wants to.Now, here's the real kicker AI lacks a coherent vision and philosophy.People don't follow creators for one piece of content they follow for their body of coherent work.You don't find value in this video because it's the first one you've watched.You find value in the entire philosophy I've strung together over the past 3, to 4 years that helps specific, creative individuals make sense of their place in the world.If you're new here, you may find some value, but it compounds the more you watch it's central pick.Or at least that's my hope.The good news is good writers or creators won't be replaced.They will become more powerful and dynamic.Artificial intelligence has allowed one person to create more faster, in a company or not, but only if they value agency and self-development.

sections.title: The Opportunity Of A Lifetime
sections.content: The ultimate goal is getting paid to be yourself.That's a previous tweet of mine to frame exactly what we're going to talk about,because most people won't like to hear this, but it's ironic that the only way out is through.To battle this processed content flood,you must contribute to humanity by posting organic content now.Contribute to humanity may sound like a strong line here, but when social media is the media now, and you are the media and media shapes culture, and culture shapes, everything from politics to the economy, something as simple as posting online can be pretty important.Now, it's my personal belief that this is one of the few ways, forward to become a value creator.And this isn't anything new.This is what the most successful and happy people have done throughout history. Why?Because happiness stems from resistance being overcome and a connection to something greater than yourself.In other words, creativity and contribution.When you create, you solve the problem. When you pass down that creation to someone else, you solve their problem.When you become a person who has value to give and then give it, consistently, you learn what it means to be happy.Now the thing is, this wasn't very accessible in the past,but evolution is creative and thus it solves problems.And this was a set of problems that's been brewing for a long time.The problems of work you hate, a lifestyle you don't control,and the lack of access to opportunity and then income, social media as a solution.And you just think of it as this platform that is extremely toxic.The good sides of social media continue to go underappreciated because one anyone can learn.You have intelligence on tap, especially with AI,you can find any information you need to achieve what you want to achieve.Two is that anyone can create you don't need permission to post, who you are, what you do, and why you do it in public.And three anyone can be discovered.You no longer need a publisher, job board or record. label to write a book, find work, or make music.Your personal brand is your public resume.But again, referring back to Kevin's tweet, if you try to build a personal brand, instead of just being yourself online, your online character, because social media, and the internet is so ingrained in our lives that that's kind of just what it is.Your personal brand, your profile on social media is just an extension of yourself,and it's an incredibly challenging one at that, because you actually have to improve.You have to get better at writing and speaking.If you want to contribute any kind of value and make any kind of independent income from your creative work,then you have to learn marketing and you have to learn business.You have to create a product.You have to be okay with people criticizing you and calling you an idiot in the comments.It's a very vulnerable position to be and it creates diamonds because of all the pressure.Now, the thing here is that social media is where the attention is. In the past, it was on things like the TV and billboards and the radio and the newspaper.The average person often didn't have the opportunity, to be in those things to promote their work.The other thing here is that AI is continuing to replace lower level jobs.And the other, other thing here is that the top paying, jobs are reserved for the elite and skill.It's the top 1%, even the top 0.1%.It's like getting into the NBA.Go look up the statistics on that.You're probably not getting into it.

sections.title: You Are The Moat (How To Future Proof Yourself)
sections.content: So the path forward, in my opinion, lies, in what Nouvel has been saying or hinting at what I've said many times in the past, that I think everyone should become an entrepreneur slash creator small business owner.The future of work has been moving toward that enough time.Just look at remote work statistics and freelance statistics.And when big companies continue to outsource their work, the low level work to AI and other things, that's a great sign.You don't have to work a job you hate anymore.You can create your own work and there's a lot more to that.It's not just a place to promote your work.The internet is a decentralized school system.Creators are the teachers who can teach relevant and useful information, whose sole purpose isn't to make you, a useful worker.Like schools, the internet is a public town square where your value can be discovered by those who need it.Building an audience is the new resume and status symbol. Followers are less about vanity and more about social proof that people find value in your ideas.And what ties this all together is that anyone can be an entrepreneur.Because when the novelty and challenge of schooling and job advancement come to a halt in your mid-twenties,causing a lack of meaning and purpose, entrepreneurship is how you continue to develop yourself and control your future.I created this graphic in the past that shows that after being in a job, for a specific amount of time, you're progress starts to plateau.The reason that you liked going to school so much and you may not have liked it.I didn't like it, but you didn't.It didn't feel boring and purposeless.You actually had something to do.You had this progression that you were going through.You were learning new things.You were improving yourself.And then you go to this job where there's a challenge and you have to learn these skills.And sure, some of it's annoying. You don't like doing a lot of it, but that's life and you increase your skill level anyways.That's where the novelty, challenge, fulfillment, and other things come from.And once it dies down and you plateau, that disappears.And then we get stuck in this repetitive and mundane robotic life,and we don't take that as a sign to branch off and do our own thing.After we've developed ourselves so much, we've learned so much, we can learn even more.We can start self educating. That's the natural path.And when that mundane and robotic life starts to set in, that's the best time to pivot off.I'm not saying don't ever work a job. Don't ever do this.I'm saying if you want to be in control of your lifestyle and the novelty challenge, meaning and fulfillment that comes from it, entrepreneurship is the only logical option for that.I'm writing an entire book on this. I'm going to make the PDF free.It'll probably launch in like February, this February or March. It's it's short. It's going to be called Purpose and Profit.And it will give you a completely new view on what entrepreneurship means.Entrepreneurship is not a role or a title.It's a state of mind.You can be an employee with the mind of an entrepreneur.That's high agency that solves problems.That's who employers want to hire that are going to be the top 1%.So even in the future, if you're working for someone else,the only logical option still is to be an entrepreneur.Now, of course, this will not sit well with some people, but listen, I'm not telling you to do anything crazy.You have all of these beliefs just stuck up in your mind that when I see these things, it's like red alarms are going off, and all of these emotions and thoughts and other things are trying to combat what I'm saying.When you haven't even met me or talk to me, I'm simply promoting the value, of self-governance for taking control of the things you can take control of. And your work and your life is one of those things, and that's pretty big.I'm showing you that there is a path to taking control of your life, in this dead internet that we're talking about, that if you were to become a creator,you wouldn't have to worry about jobs, careers, and I, as novel would say.Now realize that I'm not talking about a content creator.We've talked about this quite a bit.I'm talking about the essence of your being.You are a human, therefore you are a creator.We aren't those measly, puny little beings of the past, who look to the gods as the creators, and that we couldn't create anything ourselves, at some point in time that drastically switched and we become we became the creators.Maybe this was during the enlightenment, where progress really started to take off, and we realized we can acquire knowledge.We can solve problems, we can build tools that allow us, to not die in the freezing cold or get burned to death. And that's a mindset that so many people have yet to adopt, is that you can just create things.You can just solve problems.Yeah, it's going to be difficult, but that's where the meaning and purpose in your life comes from.And you'll never find that unless you bite the bullet, unless you just lean into the pain, the struggle,and you realize that struggle and pain in specific situations can be fun.You aren't an animal.You aren't a niche specialist that only thrives in the savanna like a lion, or Alaska like a polar bear.And if you were to switch to two, they would die.You're not that kind of you can put on a jacket because we created jackets.You can live the life you want because you can create the tools that allow you to live the life you want.The thing with this is, is that the internet is where creators go right now.That's the most accessible place.You don't need to pay any money. You don't need to be in the right location to meet the right person, to form the right network, to get the right job.To get into this, you can just post your thoughts online and get in front of almost anyone.And the thing with this is that building an audience, social media, all of these things, the internet,we still see them as something that's apart from us, that aren't us.But I want you to stop thinking about that and just think of it as a channel,a vessel to distribute your life's work, to pursue your life's work, because it's available now.That's why the internet was, I think, subconsciously, created to connect with anyone, because we're social creatures.And in order to complete your life's work, you need to be in contact, and connection with other people who benefit from the life's work.And when you do that, you're forced to learn the right skills.You're wondering, oh, what skills will be relevant 20 years from now. If you were to actually just start doing this, you would learn all of the skills that are meta, that won't be replaced, like agency, storytelling,marketing, sales, writing, not being a content writer, illustrating the story, and how to orchestrate your vision with the help of AI all as one person.It's not about cringe worthy content templates and how to grow to a billion followers in six months.Think of Jordan Peterson.He isn't a content creator.He writes books, he gives talks.He has a writing software.He has Peterson Academy because schools wouldn't cut it.The internet and social media are simply the best tools, to advance his life's work, and that will probably change.But true creators will adapt.If social media went to nothing, do you think Jordan Peterson would just be like,oh, well, I'm a content creator, so looks like my work is done here.It doesn't matter if it's intergalactic space or virtual reality,or we revert back to the Stone age where people were creators. They built and traded things.Everyone had their role, therefore they had purpose.No matter if it's any of those, the answer is to become a creator, you must solve problems and distribute solutions.Create the internet you want to be a part of.Create the content you want to see in the world.Create the products you would buy, use and benefit from.Now in the tech space, there's a lot of talk about moats,like a castle that's surrounded by a moat to protect it from its enemies, and when deep sea.The Chinese company started releasing these models like or one that rivaled, ChatGPT latest models at the fraction of a cost,and then being open source, people started to scream there is no moat.In other words, there's like open AI doesn't have all of the resources to themselves.They aren't going to be this megacorp that just controls all of the intelligence available to you, and charges up the ass for in fact, intelligence as a resource, is going to continue to go towards zero. And what that means, as some people on Twitter have been saying, is that the moat in, this case is the app layer building interfaces, building use cases for AI.And that's ironic because the people building chat, GPT wrappers or interfaces, are the ones with true security when everyone was just making fun of them.What that means is that like a software like cortex, when the AI features are in, it's like,oh, well, this can just this just has like chat GPT inside of it, which isn't the case.It has AI inside of it, but oh, it has this chat feature inside of it.I can just do this in chat. GPT why do I need cortex?You're missing the entire point because people don't realize that everything is a wrapper.Think of Typeform, the billion dollar company.If you don't know Typeform what I don't know Typeform.The online form builder is just an HTML form wrapper.Anyone can, copy paste the code of a form onto a page type form.Why are they $1 billion company?I'll let you just think that through. So that begs the question here.What's the ultimate wrapper?What's the moat? And it's you.You are the niche.Your vision, interests, experience, values, beliefs, and everything that makes you.You is the most unique wrapper of any creation you put out into the world.Right to yourself, build for yourself, solve your own problems, and sell the solution.By all measures.That is the only way to futureproof yourself.Everything else is a distraction.Now, if you want to learn my writing system to build an audience of supporters for your work, or your life's work, check out to our writer.If you want to learn the basics of modern business, check out the one person Business Launchpad.And if you want, and if you want a place to capture ideas,build a second brain and discover how unique your ideas actually are.Switch your writing and ideas over to court.Thank you for watching!Like, subscribe, comment.Let me know if you like this style of video.I can make more. Thank you for watching again. --- 由 vCaptions 生成 ---

excerpt: According to Wikipedia,the dead internet theory is a conspiracy theory suggesting that the internet died, sometime around 2016 to 2017,and is now largely dominated by bots and AI generated content rather than people.But there's something even more surprising about this theory that nobody's talking...
filename: The creator economy is dying thanks to AI.txt
